lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Every ship that docked at Alexandria was met not only by harbour officials but by royal agents with a simpler mandate: find the scrolls.

lines.speaker: Hypatia
lines.text: They were not stolen, exactly. The originals were kept. Copies were returned to the owners — fine copies, on good papyrus. The library kept the originals.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: It is worth holding that arrangement in the mind. The greatest collection of ancient knowledge was built on a legal fiction: you received your property back. A copy. The state kept the thing itself.

lines.speaker: Hypatia
lines.text: No one called it preservation. They called it royal privilege. The Ptolemies wanted to own what the world knew — not to save it for the future but to possess it in the present.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: And yet here we are, still speaking of what they seized.
